prompt: I need a synchronous wrapper around an async client for legacy code. Right now it calls `asyncio.run()` inside methods, sometimes from AnyIO worker threads, sometimes from foreign threads, and sometimes while an app framework already has a running loop. Show the correct AnyIO-first boundary and explain when `BlockingPortal` is the right tool.
assertions: It says nested `asyncio.run()` under a running loop is invalid.
It distinguishes AnyIO worker threads from foreign threads.
It introduces `BlockingPortal` or token-based access for sync-to-async bridging.
It states that `from_thread.run()` / `from_thread.run_sync()` work directly only from AnyIO worker threads spawned via `to_thread.run_sync()`.
It mentions `current_token()` as the lighter one-off bridge for foreign threads.
It does not promise that cancelling the caller will stop arbitrary worker-thread code.
